TRAIN_MAX_STEPS)\\n    else:\\n        total_steps = max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)\\n    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        if current_step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(current_step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (current_step - warmup_steps) / float(max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps))\\n        cosine = 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With a fixed 256-step budget, a step-aware warmup+cosine schedule should improve early optimization efficiency versus constant LR; pairing it with a modest LR increase is a compact, high-upside change.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
